channel.title: Jason Trost - Notes
channel.description: Shorter pieces on prediction markets, talks, and things seen up close.

item.title: Notes from the Audience at Semafor World Economy 2026
item.description: Three people I watched from the audience at Semafor World Economy 2026: Gianni Infantino, John Kerry, and Steve Bannon.
item.author: jason@smarkets.com (Jason Trost)
item.content:encoded:
Three days at Semafor World Economy 2026 in Washington, DC last week. I spoke on one panel; mostly I was there to listen.

Three people I watched from the audience I'm still thinking about: Gianni Infantino on FIFA's dynamic ticket pricing, John Kerry in a business register rather than a political one, and Steve Bannon in full conviction.

Read the full notes

item.title: Notes from Semafor World Economy 2026
item.description: Notes from my panel at Semafor World Economy 2026 on prediction markets, mention contracts, and why the industry needs an adult in the room.
item.author: jason@smarkets.com (Jason Trost)
item.content:encoded:
Last week I sat on a panel called "The Next Era of Innovation" at Semafor World Economy 2026 in Washington, DC. Yinka Adegoke interviewed me about Smarkets and prediction markets.

A few things I said that I want to write down: on whether prediction markets are gambling, on sensitive mention contracts, on where the business actually is, and on prediction markets as news infrastructure.

Read the full notes
